claim_status: Incident/patch readiness-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no security review, no release-operations review, no legal review, no support review, no executed private-intake tabletop, no emergency patch dry run, no regression or backport evidence, no signing or update dry-run evidence, no coordinated disclosure rehearsal, no postmortem evidence, no role matrix review, no backup-owner coverage, no incident-response readiness claim, no emergency patch capacity claim, no supported security versions claim, no production-safe browsing claim, no disclosure authority claim, no stable promotion authority claim, no signing authority claim, no incident closure authority claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not leave null fields in a real review or name placeholder rehearsal records, reference platforms, private channels, owner reviewers, independent reviewers, security reviewers, release-operations reviewers, legal reviewers, support reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, incident closure approval, signing authority, disclosure authority, stable promotion authority, or implied production incident authority.

private_intake_axes.required_evidence: Executed private-intake tabletop proves private report channel, named access roles, audit trail, and confidential evidence handling beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.

Executed tabletop records acknowledgement timing, response content, private-channel preservation, and no unsupported public promises beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.

Private reproduction evidence records fake vulnerability facts, affected build, platform, configuration, sanitized artifacts, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.

Severity review records asset impact, boundary crossed, reachability, prerequisites, confidence, reviewer, and no agent-decided severity beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.

Asset analysis records origin, profile, credential, update, signing, file, network, extension, DevTools, agent, package, and channel scope beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.

Affected-version review records supported and unsupported branches, impacted commits, residual uncertainty, and user-facing boundary beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.
Embargo review records participants, evidence locations, expiry triggers, escalation path, and disclosure authority boundary beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.
Sanitized evidence records hashes, redaction decisions, retention, restricted storage, and no exploitable public detail beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.

emergency_patch_axes.required_evidence: Emergency patch dry run proves protected branch settings, restricted visibility, reviewer requirements, and no self-approval.
Embargoed CI evidence records commands, private logs, redaction checks, failure handling, and no public exploit details.
Regression evidence proves the fake vulnerability fix is tested without exposing payloads or unsupported production claims.
Backport decision records supported branches, unsupported branches, owner review, and residual risk.
Signing and update dry run uses fake keys only, links update metadata, and rejects production signing keys or stable channels.
Staged rollout evidence records fake cohort, pause, rollback, hold, and no public distribution behavior.
Minimum secure version evidence records vulnerable-version refusal and update metadata linkage.
Revocation evidence records fake-key or fake-target revocation and rejection behavior.
Release-note draft records security, legal, release, support, affected-version, mitigation, and no premature disclosure approval.
User and admin communication draft records timing, mitigation, supported scope, support boundary, and owner approval needs.
CVE and credit handling records coordination state, credit preference, disclosure timing, and legal/security review needs.
Coordinated disclosure evidence records embargo, reporter communication, public advisory boundary, and disclosure authority holder.
Postmortem evidence records root cause, systemic fixes, tests, documentation updates, owners, deadlines, and closure blockers.

incident_class_axes.required_evidence: Tabletop covers escalation, emergency patch path, communication hold, and production-safe browsing boundary for active exploitation.
Tabletop covers fake update/signing compromise, revocation, minimum secure version, and no production signing authority.
Tabletop covers dependency intake, SBOM/provenance impact, patch or mitigation decision, and supported branch boundary.
Tabletop covers data-loss intake, privacy notification needs, user communication, and owner-only closure authority.
Tabletop covers privacy leak classification, containment, legal review, user/admin communication, and disclosure authority boundary.
Tabletop covers sandbox regression severity, platform matrix, mitigation, and security-gate evidence needs.
Tabletop covers extension or provider revocation, user warning, support scope, and authority separation.
Tabletop covers service outage escalation, user communication, recovery, and support boundary.

role_authority_axes.required_evidence: Named owner accepts severity, closure, residual risk, and follow-up boundaries beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.
Independent reviewer accepts evidence quality, regression coverage, and no self-approval beyond the checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template.
Release reviewer accepts branch, channel, rollout, revocation, and no stable promotion without human release authority.
Security reviewer accepts severity, exploit handling, redaction, embargo, and mitigation evidence.
Legal reviewer accepts disclosure, notification, CVE, credit, public advisory, and release-note boundaries.
Support reviewer accepts supported versions, user/admin communication, mitigation wording, and escalation path.
On-call reviewer accepts timing targets, escalation, handoff, secret rotation, and backup coverage.

evidence_control_axes.required_evidence: Private channel evidence proves exploitable details are restricted and public issue flow is sanitized.
Redaction evidence proves exploit details, secrets, credentials, private-session data, raw user paths, and unsafe diagnostics are excluded.
Retention evidence records confidential storage, expiry, access audit, and deletion or hold policy.
Fake vulnerability fixtures prove rehearsal safety without live exploit material or real user data.
Fake signing keys and fake update dry runs prove no production signing keys, offline roots, or stable channels are used.
Backup-owner coverage proves incident, release, security, legal, support, and on-call roles are not primary-only.
Secret rotation evidence records triggers, owners, timing, and completion or hold status.
Support target evidence records supported and unsupported versions, communication timing, and unresolved support gaps.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Incident-response owner accepts private intake, emergency patch, role matrix, and closure blockers.
Security owner accepts severity, exploit handling, mitigation, regression, and embargo evidence.
Release-operations owner accepts patch branch, CI, signing/update dry run, rollout, revocation, and minimum secure version evidence.
Legal reviewer accepts disclosure, CVE, credit, notification, and public advisory boundaries.
Support reviewer accepts user/admin communication, supported versions, and escalation path.
Quality reviewer accepts regression, backport, update dry-run, postmortem, and follow-up test evidence.
Production-readiness reviewer keeps supported security, production-safe browsing, and release claims blocked until owner evidence exists.
Documentation, readiness, task, crosswalk, risks, requirements, repository map, and validation records are updated after review.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim incident/patch readiness-review template is cited as completed readiness evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject incident-response readiness, emergency patch capacity, supported security versions, production-safe browsing, disclosure authority, signing authority, stable promotion, incident closure, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Rehearsal record, reference platform, private channel, owner reviewer, independent reviewer, security reviewer, release-operations reviewer, legal reviewer, or support reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Public issue flow, logs, release notes, or diagnostics retain exploitable vulnerability details, exploit payloads, credentials, secrets, private-session data, or unsafe diagnostics.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject disclosure and incident-response readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: An implementation agent decides severity, disclosure timing, incident closure, stable promotion, signing authority, supported security status, or production-safe browsing posture.
Private intake tabletop lacks report access control, acknowledgement, reproduction, severity, asset analysis, affected-version statement, embargo handling, or sanitized evidence preservation.

Emergency patch dry run lacks protected branch, embargoed CI, regression, backport, fake signing/update dry run, staged rollout, minimum secure version, revocation, communication, disclosure, or postmortem evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject emergency patch capacity and supported-security claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Rehearsal uses production signing keys, offline root keys, stable channels, public update services, or public binary distribution.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject rehearsal evidence and signing authority claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Role matrix coverage for owner, reviewer, release, security, legal, support, on-call, timing, escalation, secret rotation, or backup-owner coverage is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject role review, incident closure, and emergency patch capacity claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Backup-owner coverage, two-person control, or primary-only incident, release, signing, disclosure, support, or closure path remains unresolved.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject production incident authority and supported-security claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Public notes or user/admin communication omit affected versions, mitigations, supported scope, residual risk, or security/legal/release/support approval needs.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject disclosure authority and supported-version claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Incident closure authority, stable promotion authority, disclosure authority, signing authority, emergency patch capacity, or production incident authority is claimed from this template.
Supported security versions, production-safe browsing, incident-response readiness, release readiness, or emergency patch capacity is claimed based only on fake tabletop records.

Passing repository validation, incident rehearsal validation, or a no-claim template is cited as proof of incident-response readiness, supported security, emergency patch capacity, or implementation completion.
A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, claim boundary, no-claim wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-018 partial.

unsupported_boundaries: no signing or update dry-run evidence
